patch:
@@ -166,5 +166,13 @@ testing/playwright/test-results/.last-run.json
 backend/static/cache
 docker-compose.yaml
 
+#OPENCODE
+.opencode/                # Carpeta de skills y caché local
+opencode.json             # Tu configuración con paths absolutos
+.env                      # SIEMPRE ignora esto (aquí van las API keys)
+AGENTS.md                 # Tu archivo de contexto (si quieres que sea privado)
+CONTEXT.md
+
+
 
 ./clinerules
\ No newline at end of file

@@ -0,0 +1,1869 @@
+1
+00:00:02,700 --> 00:00:04,140
+- [Narrator] King Louie and his family
+
+2
+00:00:04,140 --> 00:00:06,330
+were now in the Tuileries Palace in Paris,
+
+3
+00:00:06,330 --> 00:00:07,920
+where for the next couple of years,
+
+4
+00:00:07,920 --> 00:00:09,630
+he watched as the revolutionary government
+
+5
+00:00:09,630 --> 00:00:11,580
+began to strip away his power
+
+6
+00:00:11,580 --> 00:00:13,110
+and fearing for his safety,
+
+7
+00:00:13,110 --> 00:00:14,640
+he had to stay on their good side.
+
+8
+00:00:14,640 --> 00:00:16,080
+Hey, look who it is.
+
+9
+00:00:16,080 --> 00:00:17,670
+It's my favorite revolutionaries.
+
+10
+00:00:17,670 --> 00:00:19,650
+Yep, I'm your number one fan.
+
+11
+00:00:19,650 --> 00:00:20,880
+What can I do for you?
+
+12
+00:00:20,880 --> 00:00:21,713
+Hey, King Louie.
+
+13
+00:00:21,713 --> 00:00:23,100
+So we've made a few decisions.
+
+14
+00:00:23,100 --> 00:00:24,900
+First, all of your friends in the nobility
+
+15
+00:00:24,900 --> 00:00:27,570
+are gonna have to pay taxes
+the same as everyone else.
+
+16
+00:00:27,570 --> 00:00:28,740
+Great idea.
+
+17
+00:00:28,740 --> 00:00:29,573
+I love it.
+
+18
+00:00:29,573 --> 00:00:30,600
+And as a side note,
+
+19
+00:00:30,600 --> 00:00:33,180
+the tax money can no longer pay
+for all your lavish parties.
+
+20
+00:00:33,180 --> 00:00:35,250
+Great, I hate those parties.
+
+21
+00:00:35,250 --> 00:00:36,390
+They're so awkward.
+
+22
+00:00:36,390 --> 00:00:38,190
+And also we're taking away your Porsche.
+
+23
+00:00:38,190 --> 00:00:39,810
+Ah, come on.
+
+24
+00:00:39,810 --> 00:00:41,400
+I mean,
+
+25
+00:00:41,400 --> 00:00:42,233
+Yay.
+
+26
+00:00:42,233 --> 00:00:44,790
+The king continually
+found demand after demand
+
+27
+00:00:44,790 --> 00:00:45,623
+being made of him
+
+28
+00:00:45,623 --> 00:00:47,400
+to prove his support for the revolution.
+
+29
+00:00:47,400 --> 00:00:48,360
+On one occasion,
+
+30
+00:00:48,360 --> 00:00:49,710
+a mob would invade the palace
+
+31
+00:00:49,710 --> 00:00:51,810
+and demand he wear the
+revolutionary bonnet.
+
+32
+00:00:51,810 --> 00:00:54,510
+This is the face of a
+man who is definitely
+
+33
+00:00:54,510 --> 00:00:56,580
+pretending he wants to wear that bonnet.
+
+34
+00:00:56,580 --> 00:00:57,413
+Now around here,
+
+35
+00:00:57,413 --> 00:00:59,580
+I wanna mention that one thing
+King Louie had a problem with
+
+36
+00:00:59,580 --> 00:01:01,650
+was people constantly raiding his palace,
+
+37
+00:01:01,650 --> 00:01:03,330
+but one thing he didn't
+have a problem with
+
+38
+00:01:03,330 --> 00:01:04,380
+was raiding noobs
+
+39
+00:01:04,380 --> 00:01:07,500
+on this video's sponsor,
+Raid Shadow Legends.
+
+40
+00:01:07,500 --> 00:01:10,380
+Raid Shadow Legends is a
+brand new collection RPG game
+
+41
+00:01:10,380 --> 00:01:13,110
+with amazing 3D graphics
+and awesome storyline
+
+42
+00:01:13,110 --> 00:01:14,310
+and giant boss fights
+
+43
+00:01:14,310 --> 00:01:16,410
+all free to play on your phone.
+
+44
+00:01:16,410 --> 00:01:18,570
+There's over 400 champions
+for you to collect
+
+45
+00:01:18,570 --> 00:01:19,740
+and customize
+
+46
+00:01:19,740 --> 00:01:21,120
+and crazy battles and events
+
+47
+00:01:21,120 --> 00:01:22,920
+that will get you hooked immediately.
+
+48
+00:01:22,920 --> 00:01:24,150
+You can find me in the game
+
+49
+00:01:24,150 --> 00:01:26,100
+under the nickname OverSimplified.
+
+50
+00:01:26,100 --> 00:01:27,990
+And if you're quick
+enough and cool enough,
+
+51
+00:01:27,990 --> 00:01:29,160
+you can join my clan.
+
+52
+00:01:29,160 --> 00:01:30,600
+Go to the video description,
+
+53
+00:01:30,600 --> 00:01:31,830
+click on the special links
+
+54
+00:01:31,830 --> 00:01:34,110
+and you will instantly get 50,000 silver
+
+55
+00:01:34,110 --> 00:01:35,760
+and a free epic champion
+
+56
+00:01:35,760 --> 00:01:37,560
+as part of the new player program
+
+57
+00:01:37,560 --> 00:01:38,970
+to start your journey.
+
+58
+00:01:38,970 --> 00:01:40,653
+Good luck and I'll see you there.
+
+59
+00:01:41,640 --> 00:01:42,490
+Now, where was I?
+
+60
+00:01:43,470 --> 00:01:44,303
+Oh yeah.
+
+61
+00:01:44,303 --> 00:01:46,650
+Seeing the situation
+rapidly turning against him,
+
+62
+00:01:46,650 --> 00:01:49,530
+the king decided it might be
+a good idea to leave France
+
+63
+00:01:49,530 --> 00:01:52,230
+and mount a campaign to retake
+his country from abroad.
+
+64
+00:01:52,230 --> 00:01:53,400
+Luckily for him,
+
+65
+00:01:53,400 --> 00:01:54,930
+he was married to an Austrian.
+
+66
+00:01:54,930 --> 00:01:57,570
+So on the night of June 20th, 1791,
+
+67
+00:01:57,570 --> 00:02:00,210
+the king in his family
+disguised themselves as servants
+
+68
+00:02:00,210 --> 00:02:02,910
+and attempted to flee to
+the Austrian Netherlands.
+
+69
+00:02:02,910 --> 00:02:05,370
+The royal carriage made a
+stop in the town of Varennes
+
+70
+00:02:05,370 --> 00:02:06,847
+and the postmaster there was like,
+
+71
+00:02:06,847 --> 00:02:08,130
+"hey guys, what's up?
+
+72
+00:02:08,130 --> 00:02:09,060
+Where are you off to?"
+
+73
+00:02:09,060 --> 00:02:09,960
+We are but a collection
+
+74
+00:02:09,960 --> 00:02:11,970
+of inconspicuous servants
+heading for the border
+
+75
+00:02:11,970 --> 00:02:13,890
+for no particular reason at all.
+
+76
+00:02:13,890 --> 00:02:17,040
+Say, you, the fat one.
+
+77
+00:02:17,040 --> 00:02:18,750
+You look kind of familiar.
+
+78
+00:02:18,750 --> 00:02:20,070
+Aren't you the king?
+
+79
+00:02:20,070 --> 00:02:20,910
+Nope.
+
+80
+00:02:20,910 --> 00:02:22,920
+Let me see your passport.
+
+81
+00:02:22,920 --> 00:02:27,810
+It says here, you're King Louis the 16th.
+
+82
+00:02:27,810 --> 00:02:29,373
+Nope, not me.
+
+83
+00:02:31,920 --> 00:02:32,940
+Take him away, boys.
+
+84
+00:02:32,940 --> 00:02:34,980
+The King was promptly returned to Paris,
+
+85
+00:02:34,980 --> 00:02:36,720
+but now the jig was up.
+
+86
+00:02:36,720 --> 00:02:38,490
+His lack of support for the revolution
+
+87
+00:02:38,490 --> 00:02:39,960
+was clear to all
+
+88
+00:02:39,960 --> 00:02:42,210
+and many considered him
+a straight up traitor
+
+89
+00:02:42,210 --> 00:02:44,100
+who tried to abandon his people.
+
+90
+00:02:44,100 --> 00:02:44,933
+As a result,
+
+91
+00:02:44,933 --> 00:02:48,300
+the new Constitution of 1791
+completely reduced his powers
+
+92
+00:02:48,300 --> 00:02:49,800
+to that of a simple figurehead,
+
+93
+00:02:49,800 --> 00:02:51,630
+a constitutional monarch.
+
+94
+00:02:51,630 --> 00:02:53,070
+However radicals,
+
+95
+00:02:53,070 --> 00:02:54,720
+such as those in the Jacobin Club,
+
+96
+00:02:54,720 --> 00:02:57,810
+were outraged that the king
+wasn't to be removed entirely.
+
+97
+00:02:57,810 --> 00:02:58,710
+So a month later,
+
+98
+00:02:58,710 --> 00:03:00,240
+these radicals staged a protest
+
+99
+00:03:00,240 --> 00:03:03,090
+on the Champ de Mars calling
+for the king's removal.
+
+100
+00:03:03,090 --> 00:03:05,850
+The government of Paris feared
+an insurrection was mounting
+
+101
+00:03:05,850 --> 00:03:08,160
+and they sent the military
+to disperse the crowd.
+
+102
+00:03:08,160 --> 00:03:09,690
+The confrontation escalated
+
+103
+00:03:09,690 --> 00:03:12,000
+and resulted in the
+revolutionary National Guard
+
+104
+00:03:12,000 --> 00:03:14,700
+firing on a crowd of revolutionaries.
+
+105
+00:03:14,700 --> 00:03:16,470
+It was a massacre.
+
+106
+00:03:16,470 --> 00:03:18,750
+The incident exposed a deep division
+
+107
+00:03:18,750 --> 00:03:20,880
+within the brotherhood of the revolution.
+
+108
+00:03:20,880 --> 00:03:21,713
+On one side,
+
+109
+00:03:21,713 --> 00:03:23,220
+the moderates who wanted to keep the King
+
+110
+00:03:23,220 --> 00:03:24,270
+as a figurehead,
+
+111
+00:03:24,270 --> 00:03:27,240
+on the other radicals who
+wanted to see the king deposed
+
+112
+00:03:27,240 --> 00:03:28,380
+and heads roll.
+
+113
+00:03:28,380 --> 00:03:29,850
+In the wake of the massacre,
+
+114
+00:03:29,850 --> 00:03:32,850
+these radicals received a wave of support.
+
+115
+00:03:32,850 --> 00:03:34,380
+And speaking of rolling heads,
+
+116
+00:03:34,380 --> 00:03:36,750
+one form of equality of
+the revolution introduced
+
+117
+00:03:36,750 --> 00:03:38,580
+was a quality in execution.
+
+118
+00:03:38,580 --> 00:03:40,740
+This meant no more torturous
+drawing and quartering,
+
+119
+00:03:40,740 --> 00:03:42,090
+no more inhumane hanging.
+
+120
+00:03:42,090 --> 00:03:43,440
+They wanted all criminals,
+
+121
+00:03:43,440 --> 00:03:46,740
+regardless of economic status
+to receive the same penalty.
+
+122
+00:03:46,740 --> 00:03:48,270
+A quick and painless one.
+
+123
+00:03:48,270 --> 00:03:51,030
+Luckily, a man by the name
+of Dr. Joseph Guillotine
+
+124
+00:03:51,030 --> 00:03:52,110
+had an idea.
+
+125
+00:03:52,110 --> 00:03:54,000
+A heavy blade that falls like thunder.
+
+126
+00:03:54,000 --> 00:03:55,980
+The head flies off, blood spurts,
+
+127
+00:03:55,980 --> 00:03:57,270
+and the man is no more.
+
+128
+00:03:57,270 --> 00:04:00,090
+The guillotine, otherwise
+known as the National Razor.
+
+129
+00:04:00,090 --> 00:04:02,610
+The guillotine made its debut in 1791
+
+130
+00:04:02,610 --> 00:04:04,560
+as the new form of execution.
+
+131
+00:04:04,560 --> 00:04:05,640
+The writings of Marat
+
+132
+00:04:05,640 --> 00:04:07,830
+and others continued to
+call for the execution
+
+133
+00:04:07,830 --> 00:04:09,330
+of anyone suspected of working
+
+134
+00:04:09,330 --> 00:04:10,740
+against the revolution.
+
+135
+00:04:10,740 --> 00:04:11,573
+For him,
+
+136
+00:04:11,573 --> 00:04:13,830
+this included some members
+of the clergy and nobility
+
+137
+00:04:13,830 --> 00:04:15,000
+who had previously benefited
+
+138
+00:04:15,000 --> 00:04:16,710
+from the cruel system of inequality
+
+139
+00:04:16,710 --> 00:04:18,660
+that existed before the revolution.
+
+140
+00:04:18,660 --> 00:04:20,190
+In many parts of the countryside,
+
+141
+00:04:20,190 --> 00:04:22,590
+local lords had found
+themselves become a target.
+
+142
+00:04:22,590 --> 00:04:25,080
+Sire, the peasants, they're revolting.
+
+143
+00:04:25,080 --> 00:04:25,913
+Oh, come on.
+
+144
+00:04:25,913 --> 00:04:26,746
+That's a bit harsh.
+
+145
+00:04:26,746 --> 00:04:27,579
+Sure, they smell a bit,
+
+146
+00:04:27,579 --> 00:04:29,030
+but I wouldn't say they're revolting.
+
+147
+00:04:29,940 --> 00:04:31,530
+Oh, yes, I see what you mean.
+
+148
+00:04:31,530 --> 00:04:33,360
+Increasingly, these French aristocrats
+
+149
+00:04:33,360 --> 00:04:35,250
+began fleeing France to find solitude
+
+150
+00:04:35,250 --> 00:04:36,510
+in other parts of Europe.
+
+151
+00:04:36,510 --> 00:04:37,440
+And once again,
+
+152
+00:04:37,440 --> 00:04:39,090
+fear began to take hold.
+
+153
+00:04:39,090 --> 00:04:41,310
+The privileged classes
+of these foreign nations
+
+154
+00:04:41,310 --> 00:04:42,420
+didn't like what they were seeing
+
+155
+00:04:42,420 --> 00:04:44,220
+because they feared revolutionary ideas
+
+156
+00:04:44,220 --> 00:04:45,810
+may spread to their own lands.
+
+157
+00:04:45,810 --> 00:04:47,010
+The National Assembly,
+
+158
+00:04:47,010 --> 00:04:48,990
+actually now the Legislative Assembly
+
+159
+00:04:48,990 --> 00:04:51,780
+feared that these nations
+may decide to attack.
+
+160
+00:04:51,780 --> 00:04:53,820
+Then why don't we attack them first?
+
+161
+00:04:53,820 --> 00:04:54,653
+No, you idiots.
+
+162
+00:04:54,653 --> 00:04:56,630
+We are definitely not ready for war yet.
+
+163
+00:04:58,440 --> 00:04:59,340
+Did somebody say something?
+
+164
+00:04:59,340 --> 00:05:02,730
+France declared war in
+Austria in April, 1792
+
+165
+00:05:02,730 --> 00:05:04,200
+and immediately got pummeled.
+
+166
+00:05:04,200 --> 00:05:06,600
+It also didn't help that
+Austria's ally, Prussia,
+
+167
+00:05:06,600 --> 00:05:07,590
+joined in the fighting.
+
+168
+00:05:07,590 --> 00:05:08,760
+The Prussian Duke of Brunswick
+
+169
+00:05:08,760 --> 00:05:10,740
+posted a letter warning
+the revolutionaries
+
+170
+00:05:10,740 --> 00:05:12,450
+that if anything happened to the King,
+
+171
+00:05:12,450 --> 00:05:14,550
+he would burn Paris to the ground.
+
+172
+00:05:14,550 --> 00:05:17,310
+The Duke's letter proved
+to be a massive success
+
+173
+00:05:17,310 --> 00:05:18,840
+in inspiring the people of Paris
+
+174
+00:05:18,840 --> 00:05:21,330
+to do the exact opposite
+of what he intended.
+
+175
+00:05:21,330 --> 00:05:22,920
+They were enraged by the threat.
+
+176
+00:05:22,920 --> 00:05:25,050
+And on the 10th of August, 1792,
+
+177
+00:05:25,050 --> 00:05:26,940
+the tension in the city exploded
+
+178
+00:05:26,940 --> 00:05:28,830
+and a mob stormed the king's palace.
+
+179
+00:05:28,830 --> 00:05:30,630
+Fighting broke out between
+the revolutionaries
+
+180
+00:05:30,630 --> 00:05:32,130
+and the king's Swiss guard
+
+181
+00:05:32,130 --> 00:05:33,900
+with casualties in the 100s.
+
+182
+00:05:33,900 --> 00:05:34,733
+King Louis fled
+
+183
+00:05:34,733 --> 00:05:37,500
+and took refuge in the chamber
+of the legislative assembly
+
+184
+00:05:37,500 --> 00:05:39,480
+where Robespierre and his radical Jacobins
+
+185
+00:05:39,480 --> 00:05:40,980
+were gaining evermore power.
+
+186
+00:05:40,980 --> 00:05:42,630
+Given the developing situation,
+
+187
+00:05:42,630 --> 00:05:44,460
+the chamber decided to hold a vote.
+
+188
+00:05:44,460 --> 00:05:47,040
+And in what some considered
+to be a second revolution,
+
+189
+00:05:47,040 --> 00:05:50,130
+it was decided to suspend
+the monarchy entirely.
+
+190
+00:05:50,130 --> 00:05:54,000
+King Louis the 16th was
+now just plain old Louis
+
+191
+00:05:54,000 --> 00:05:55,560
+and he was sent to a prison cell
+
+192
+00:05:55,560 --> 00:05:57,270
+where an eye could be kept on him.
+
+193
+00:05:57,270 --> 00:05:58,103
+A month later,
+
+194
+00:05:58,103 --> 00:06:00,060
+the newly established national convention
+
+195
+00:06:00,060 --> 00:06:02,190
+officially declared the French Republic
+
+196
+00:06:02,190 --> 00:06:04,830
+and society underwent a massive change.
+
+197
+00:06:04,830 --> 00:06:06,510
+Enlightened ideas of democracy
+
+198
+00:06:06,510 --> 00:06:08,340
+and equality were being implemented.
+
+199
+00:06:08,340 --> 00:06:09,210
+But very quickly,
+
+200
+00:06:09,210 --> 00:06:12,000
+these ideas seemed to
+become secondary to fear,
+
+201
+00:06:12,000 --> 00:06:14,400
+paranoia and a thirst for blood.
+
+202
+00:06:14,400 --> 00:06:16,710
+The new republic began
+working to violently remove
+
+203
+00:06:16,710 --> 00:06:19,050
+any semblance of the old royalist regime.
+
+204
+00:06:19,050 --> 00:06:20,760
+The church became a prime target.
+
+205
+00:06:20,760 --> 00:06:22,980
+Priests who refused to take
+a oath to the revolution
+
+206
+00:06:22,980 --> 00:06:24,600
+were deported or arrested.
+
+207
+00:06:24,600 --> 00:06:26,910
+A new state-sponsored atheistic religion
+
+208
+00:06:26,910 --> 00:06:28,470
+named the Cult of Reason was created
+
+209
+00:06:28,470 --> 00:06:30,360
+as a replacement for the Catholic Church.
+
+210
+00:06:30,360 --> 00:06:32,580
+Notre Dam, along with many other churches,
+
+211
+00:06:32,580 --> 00:06:34,440
+had the religious treasures destroyed
+
+212
+00:06:34,440 --> 00:06:36,480
+and were converted to temples of reason.
+
+213
+00:06:36,480 --> 00:06:38,520
+Even the Christian
+calendar didn't survive,
+
+214
+00:06:38,520 --> 00:06:41,520
+as a brand new revolutionary
+calendar was soon introduced.
+
+215
+00:06:41,520 --> 00:06:42,930
+Hey honey, I'm home.
+
+216
+00:06:42,930 --> 00:06:44,280
+Yeah, whatever jerk.
+
+217
+00:06:44,280 --> 00:06:45,630
+Whoa, what's wrong with you?
+
+218
+00:06:45,630 --> 00:06:46,980
+You forgot.
+
+219
+00:06:46,980 --> 00:06:48,030
+Forgot what?
+
+220
+00:06:48,030 --> 00:06:48,863
+Everything.
+
+221
+00:06:48,863 --> 00:06:50,280
+This entire year,
+
+222
+00:06:50,280 --> 00:06:52,050
+my birthday was on the third of Germinal.
+
+223
+00:06:52,050 --> 00:06:54,150
+Our anniversary was the 12th of Thermidor
+
+224
+00:06:54,150 --> 00:06:55,860
+and you promised that in Frimaire,
+
+225
+00:06:55,860 --> 00:06:57,930
+we'd go on a romantic
+weekend trip to Venice.
+
+226
+00:06:57,930 --> 00:07:00,180
+No, I said would do that in December.
+
+227
+00:07:00,180 --> 00:07:03,450
+December hasn't been a thing for years.
+
+228
+00:07:03,450 --> 00:07:04,650
+The government of Paris
+
+229
+00:07:04,650 --> 00:07:07,020
+now under the control of
+the radical San-Culottes
+
+230
+00:07:07,020 --> 00:07:09,810
+began rounding up suspected
+enemies of the revolution
+
+231
+00:07:09,810 --> 00:07:12,240
+and sending them to prison in the 1000s.
+
+232
+00:07:12,240 --> 00:07:14,250
+Naturally, a large
+number of those arrested
+
+233
+00:07:14,250 --> 00:07:16,950
+were members of the
+clergy and aristocracy.
+
+234
+00:07:16,950 --> 00:07:19,530
+As Francis foreign enemies
+continued to close in,
+
+235
+00:07:19,530 --> 00:07:20,580
+panic spread.
+
+236
+00:07:20,580 --> 00:07:22,440
+Georges Danton made impassioned calls
+
+237
+00:07:22,440 --> 00:07:24,000
+for men to defend the republic
+
+238
+00:07:24,000 --> 00:07:25,350
+and tens of 1000s of troops
+
+239
+00:07:25,350 --> 00:07:27,120
+left Paris for the front lines.
+
+240
+00:07:27,120 --> 00:07:28,740
+However, in their absence,
+
+241
+00:07:28,740 --> 00:07:30,900
+Paris was left to its own devices.
+
+242
+00:07:30,900 --> 00:07:32,730
+As enemy troops arrived in Verdun,
+
+243
+00:07:32,730 --> 00:07:34,980
+the people of Paris feared
+that their crowded prisons
+
+244
+00:07:34,980 --> 00:07:36,240
+were becoming a breeding ground
+
+245
+00:07:36,240 --> 00:07:38,700
+for counterrevolutionary conspiracy.
+
+246
+00:07:38,700 --> 00:07:40,920
+What would happen if the
+Prussians reached Paris
+
+247
+00:07:40,920 --> 00:07:42,300
+and freed the aristocrats?
+
+248
+00:07:42,300 --> 00:07:44,190
+Marat believed he knew what would happen.
+
+249
+00:07:44,190 --> 00:07:46,350
+The aristocrats would
+enact their vengeance
+
+250
+00:07:46,350 --> 00:07:47,250
+on the people.
+
+251
+00:07:47,250 --> 00:07:49,320
+Fearing those they had already imprisoned,
+
+252
+00:07:49,320 --> 00:07:51,750
+mobs descended on Paris's prisons.
+
+253
+00:07:51,750 --> 00:07:52,583
+They broke in.
+
+254
+00:07:52,583 --> 00:07:54,990
+And during the brutal September massacres,
+
+255
+00:07:54,990 --> 00:07:57,510
+aristocrats, priests and others
+
+256
+00:07:57,510 --> 00:08:00,510
+were tried and executed on the spot.
+
+257
+00:08:00,510 --> 00:08:03,180
+Even women and children weren't spared.
+
+258
+00:08:03,180 --> 00:08:05,490
+With over 1,600 victims,
+
+259
+00:08:05,490 --> 00:08:07,800
+word of the massacre spread across Europe.
+
+260
+00:08:07,800 --> 00:08:09,390
+One British newspaper wondered,
+
+261
+00:08:09,390 --> 00:08:10,830
+are these the rights of men?
+
+262
+00:08:10,830 --> 00:08:13,020
+Is this the liberty of human nature?
+
+263
+00:08:13,020 --> 00:08:14,730
+But there was still one man in particular
+
+264
+00:08:14,730 --> 00:08:16,350
+that Robespierre and his radicals
+
+265
+00:08:16,350 --> 00:08:18,030
+really wanted to see executed.
+
+266
+00:08:18,030 --> 00:08:19,800
+Austria and Prussia pledged that
+
+267
+00:08:19,800 --> 00:08:21,240
+after they defeated France,
+
+268
+00:08:21,240 --> 00:08:23,040
+they'd returned King Louis to the throne.
+
+269
+00:08:23,040 --> 00:08:24,750
+Well checkmate Austria and Prussia
+
+270
+00:08:24,750 --> 00:08:26,700
+because he can't return
+a man to the throne
+
+271
+00:08:26,700 --> 00:08:27,780
+if he's already dead.
+
+272
+00:08:27,780 --> 00:08:30,840
+Citizen Louis Capet was
+put on trial for treason.
+
+273
+00:08:30,840 --> 00:08:32,430
+Obviously he was found guilty,
+
+274
+00:08:32,430 --> 00:08:34,230
+but his punishment was less certain.
+
+275
+00:08:34,230 --> 00:08:36,540
+Many moderates wanted
+to simply deport him.
+
+276
+00:08:36,540 --> 00:08:39,480
+But Robespierre insisted the
+revolution could only live
+
+277
+00:08:39,480 --> 00:08:41,280
+if the king was dead.
+
+278
+00:08:41,280 --> 00:08:43,530
+A vote was held and by just one vote,
+
+279
+00:08:43,530 --> 00:08:45,960
+Louis was sentenced to the guillotine.
+
+280
+00:08:45,960 --> 00:08:46,800
+If you don't mind,
+
+281
+00:08:46,800 --> 00:08:48,720
+I'd like to say a few words first.
+
+282
+00:08:48,720 --> 00:08:50,910
+Gentlemen, I am innocent of everything
+
+283
+00:08:50,910 --> 00:08:51,930
+of which I am accused...
+
+284
+00:08:51,930 --> 00:08:53,160
+Wait, you're too loud.
+
+285
+00:08:53,160 --> 00:08:54,060
+They can't hear me.
+
+286
+00:08:54,060 --> 00:08:55,740
+Hang on, I haven't finished yet.
+
+287
+00:08:55,740 --> 00:08:56,633
+Wait, dude.
+
+288
+00:09:00,580 --> 00:09:01,860
+Uncool.
+
+289
+00:09:01,860 --> 00:09:03,150
+In her prison cell,
+
+290
+00:09:03,150 --> 00:09:05,070
+Marie Antoinette heard the guns fire
+
+291
+00:09:05,070 --> 00:09:07,260
+signaling her husband's death.
+
+292
+00:09:07,260 --> 00:09:08,430
+Before long,
+
+293
+00:09:08,430 --> 00:09:10,890
+she would meet the same fate.
+
+294
+00:09:10,890 --> 00:09:12,000
+Back on the war front,
+
+295
+00:09:12,000 --> 00:09:13,770
+France defied all expectations,
+
+296
+00:09:13,770 --> 00:09:15,990
+and actually managed
+to push the enemy back.
+
+297
+00:09:15,990 --> 00:09:17,610
+But then more countries
+joined the coalition
+
+298
+00:09:17,610 --> 00:09:19,650
+against France and it
+all went to pot again.
+
+299
+00:09:19,650 --> 00:09:20,550
+What do we do?
+
+300
+00:09:20,550 --> 00:09:22,200
+Conscript the masses.
+
+301
+00:09:22,200 --> 00:09:24,780
+The National Convention
+introduced a conscription law
+
+302
+00:09:24,780 --> 00:09:26,010
+with each regional department
+
+303
+00:09:26,010 --> 00:09:28,530
+having to meet a certain
+quota of men for the army.
+
+304
+00:09:28,530 --> 00:09:31,350
+However, not everyone was
+happy with this new law.
+
+305
+00:09:31,350 --> 00:09:33,720
+You see, while Paris
+was definitely a hotbed
+
+306
+00:09:33,720 --> 00:09:35,700
+for radical revolutionary fervor,
+
+307
+00:09:35,700 --> 00:09:37,320
+some of the regions outside of Paris
+
+308
+00:09:37,320 --> 00:09:39,420
+weren't quite so keen on the revolution.
+
+309
+00:09:39,420 --> 00:09:41,250
+Some were largely still conservative,
+
+310
+00:09:41,250 --> 00:09:42,630
+still supported the church
+
+311
+00:09:42,630 --> 00:09:44,850
+and just didn't suffer
+from that much inequality
+
+312
+00:09:44,850 --> 00:09:45,930
+before the revolution.
+
+313
+00:09:45,930 --> 00:09:48,150
+So as the revolution
+turned increasingly violent
+
+314
+00:09:48,150 --> 00:09:50,910
+and anti-Christian, many were outraged.
+
+315
+00:09:50,910 --> 00:09:53,010
+Now they were being conscripted to fight
+
+316
+00:09:53,010 --> 00:09:55,050
+for the new republic they hated.
+
+317
+00:09:55,050 --> 00:09:57,150
+That was the last straw.
+
+318
+00:09:57,150 --> 00:09:59,160
+Counterrevolutionary uprisings erupted
+
+319
+00:09:59,160 --> 00:10:01,350
+in a number of regions across France.
+
+320
+00:10:01,350 --> 00:10:02,970
+Some would last for years,
+
+321
+00:10:02,970 --> 00:10:04,320
+such as in the Northwest
+
+322
+00:10:04,320 --> 00:10:07,140
+where a large scale uprising
+was led by The Owls.
+
+323
+00:10:07,140 --> 00:10:08,760
+Why were they called The Owls?
+
+324
+00:10:08,760 --> 00:10:10,920
+Because their leader was named Jean Owl.
+
+325
+00:10:10,920 --> 00:10:12,360
+Why was he called John Owl?
+
+326
+00:10:12,360 --> 00:10:13,440
+Possibly because he could do
+
+327
+00:10:13,440 --> 00:10:15,330
+a really good impression of an owl.
+
+328
+00:10:15,330 --> 00:10:16,163
+Really?
+
+329
+00:10:16,163 --> 00:10:17,400
+That's what we're going with?
+
+330
+00:10:17,400 --> 00:10:18,233
+Owls?
+
+331
+00:10:18,233 --> 00:10:20,610
+Just because this guy can
+do an impression of one?
+
+332
+00:10:20,610 --> 00:10:22,020
+Hit him with it, Jean.
+
+333
+00:10:22,020 --> 00:10:22,853
+Hoot, hoot.
+
+334
+00:10:24,060 --> 00:10:24,893
+Yeah, okay.
+
+335
+00:10:24,893 --> 00:10:25,726
+That's pretty good.
+
+336
+00:10:28,170 --> 00:10:31,920
+The Chouanary uprising lasted
+all the way until 1800.
+
+337
+00:10:31,920 --> 00:10:33,630
+In the summer of 1793,
+
+338
+00:10:33,630 --> 00:10:36,000
+the southern city of Toulon
+invited the British Navy
+
+339
+00:10:36,000 --> 00:10:37,800
+over for some tea and crumpets.
+
+340
+00:10:37,800 --> 00:10:39,750
+And then they asked if
+they'd possibly like to stay
+
+341
+00:10:39,750 --> 00:10:40,860
+and occupy the city.
+
+342
+00:10:40,860 --> 00:10:42,330
+Being an important naval base,
+
+343
+00:10:42,330 --> 00:10:44,160
+this was a heavy blow to the republic
+
+344
+00:10:44,160 --> 00:10:46,290
+who sent a relatively
+unknown young captain
+
+345
+00:10:46,290 --> 00:10:47,940
+by the name of Napoleon Bonaparte
+
+346
+00:10:47,940 --> 00:10:49,620
+to help stage the siege of the city.
+
+347
+00:10:49,620 --> 00:10:52,020
+Toulon was recaptured
+by France in the winter
+
+348
+00:10:52,020 --> 00:10:53,220
+and for his service
+
+349
+00:10:53,220 --> 00:10:56,310
+Napoleon was promoted to the
+rank of brigadier general.
+
+350
+00:10:56,310 --> 00:10:58,530
+The most infamous
+counterrevolution, however,
+
+351
+00:10:58,530 --> 00:11:00,210
+occurred in the Vendee region.
+
+352
+00:11:00,210 --> 00:11:01,710
+Throughout 1793,
+
+353
+00:11:01,710 --> 00:11:03,960
+revolutionary forces
+clashed with the region's
+
+354
+00:11:03,960 --> 00:11:05,730
+Catholic and royal army.
+
+355
+00:11:05,730 --> 00:11:07,590
+The republic defeated
+the counterrevolution
+
+356
+00:11:07,590 --> 00:11:09,510
+through cruel pacification.
+
+357
+00:11:09,510 --> 00:11:10,440
+In particular,
+
+358
+00:11:10,440 --> 00:11:12,150
+general Jean Baptist Carrier
+
+359
+00:11:12,150 --> 00:11:14,340
+committed brutal atrocities.
+
+360
+00:11:14,340 --> 00:11:15,390
+In one instance,
+
+361
+00:11:15,390 --> 00:11:17,760
+he had 1000s of civilians, priests,
+
+362
+00:11:17,760 --> 00:11:20,220
+women and children tied to ships,
+
+363
+00:11:20,220 --> 00:11:21,900
+which were then sunk.
+
+364
+00:11:21,900 --> 00:11:24,720
+Carrier would later be
+found guilty of war crimes
+
+365
+00:11:24,720 --> 00:11:25,623
+and executed.
+
+366
+00:11:26,760 --> 00:11:27,780
+Back in Paris,
+
+367
+00:11:27,780 --> 00:11:30,330
+the government was still
+dominated by moderates.
+
+368
+00:11:30,330 --> 00:11:31,590
+With the war going badly,
+
+369
+00:11:31,590 --> 00:11:34,530
+revolts in the provinces and
+the economy getting worse,
+
+370
+00:11:34,530 --> 00:11:37,380
+it seemed the government just
+wasn't doing a very good job.
+
+371
+00:11:37,380 --> 00:11:40,650
+Radicals' fear for the safety
+of the revolution intensified
+
+372
+00:11:40,650 --> 00:11:42,870
+and Marat even began
+calling for the moderates
+
+373
+00:11:42,870 --> 00:11:44,910
+in the government to be executed.
+
+374
+00:11:44,910 --> 00:11:45,743
+In return,
+
+375
+00:11:45,743 --> 00:11:47,850
+the moderates called
+for the arrest of Marat.
+
+376
+00:11:47,850 --> 00:11:49,170
+This led to a chain of events
+
+377
+00:11:49,170 --> 00:11:51,150
+with the two sides in heated conflict.
+
+378
+00:11:51,150 --> 00:11:54,060
+Robespierre declared the
+Jacobins to be be an insurrection
+
+379
+00:11:54,060 --> 00:11:56,310
+and called on the people
+to arm themselves.
+
+380
+00:11:56,310 --> 00:11:59,430
+It all ended on the 31st of May, 1793
+
+381
+00:11:59,430 --> 00:12:01,050
+with the National Convention surrounded
+
+382
+00:12:01,050 --> 00:12:02,460
+by radical Sans-culottes
+
+383
+00:12:02,460 --> 00:12:06,090
+and 29 moderate Girondin
+politicians arrested.
+
+384
+00:12:06,090 --> 00:12:07,290
+From this moment on,
+
+385
+00:12:07,290 --> 00:12:09,540
+the moderate ceased to
+be a political force.
+
+386
+00:12:09,540 --> 00:12:11,280
+Robespierre and his radicals
+
+387
+00:12:11,280 --> 00:12:14,040
+would be an almost total
+control of the government.
+
+388
+00:12:14,040 --> 00:12:15,480
+And this brings us to the story
+
+389
+00:12:15,480 --> 00:12:17,310
+of a woman named Charlotte Corday.
+
+390
+00:12:17,310 --> 00:12:19,800
+Charlotte lived in the
+northwest city of Caen
+
+391
+00:12:19,800 --> 00:12:21,030
+and like many in the area
+
+392
+00:12:21,030 --> 00:12:23,460
+was horrified at the rapid radicalization
+
+393
+00:12:23,460 --> 00:12:25,710
+and increasing violence of the revolution.
+
+394
+00:12:25,710 --> 00:12:27,930
+And the man she blamed more than anyone
+
+395
+00:12:27,930 --> 00:12:29,790
+was Jean Paul Marat.
+
+396
+00:12:29,790 --> 00:12:31,860
+She wanted to bring peace back to France.
+
+397
+00:12:31,860 --> 00:12:33,570
+And so she did something drastic.
+
+398
+00:12:33,570 --> 00:12:35,400
+She traveled to Paris and told Marat
+
+399
+00:12:35,400 --> 00:12:38,250
+she had a list of enemies for
+him to publish in his paper.
+
+400
+00:12:38,250 --> 00:12:40,710
+Marat eagerly invited
+her in for a meeting.
+
+401
+00:12:40,710 --> 00:12:42,870
+So where's that list of
+enemies you promised me?
+
+402
+00:12:42,870 --> 00:12:43,980
+Here it is.
+
+403
+00:12:43,980 --> 00:12:45,330
+Wait a minute.
+
+404
+00:12:45,330 --> 00:12:46,950
+This isn't the list of enemies.
+
+405
+00:12:46,950 --> 00:12:49,929
+It just says "yippee ki yay mother..."
+
+406
+00:12:49,929 --> 00:12:50,970
+(Marat screams)
+
+407
+00:12:50,970 --> 00:12:52,110
+And just like that,
+
+408
+00:12:52,110 --> 00:12:54,060
+Marat was no more.
+
+409
+00:12:54,060 --> 00:12:56,970
+Charlotte was quickly arrested
+and sent to the guillotine.
+
+410
+00:12:56,970 --> 00:13:00,210
+Her dream of restoring peace,
+however, died with her.
+
+411
+00:13:00,210 --> 00:13:01,560
+Marat became a martyr.
+
+412
+00:13:01,560 --> 00:13:02,580
+In temples of reason,
+
+413
+00:13:02,580 --> 00:13:04,050
+symbols of the dead Marat
+
+414
+00:13:04,050 --> 00:13:05,670
+became the new crucifix.
+
+415
+00:13:05,670 --> 00:13:06,503
+In death,
+
+416
+00:13:06,503 --> 00:13:08,670
+he became an even more
+powerful inspiration
+
+417
+00:13:08,670 --> 00:13:10,320
+for the extreme levels of violence
+
+418
+00:13:10,320 --> 00:13:13,020
+that were about to rip
+throughout the new republic.
+
+419
+00:13:13,020 --> 00:13:13,920
+And that's right.
+
+420
+00:13:13,920 --> 00:13:16,530
+Here comes the reign of terror.
+
+421
+00:13:16,530 --> 00:13:18,090
+If you thought this revolution already
+
+422
+00:13:18,090 --> 00:13:19,260
+sounds pretty violent,
+
+423
+00:13:19,260 --> 00:13:21,030
+well you ain't seen nothing yet, son.
+
+424
+00:13:21,030 --> 00:13:22,800
+The radicals were now in control
+
+425
+00:13:22,800 --> 00:13:23,633
+and they believed
+
+426
+00:13:23,633 --> 00:13:25,740
+not only was France
+surrounded by foreign enemies,
+
+427
+00:13:25,740 --> 00:13:27,090
+but that within the masses,
+
+428
+00:13:27,090 --> 00:13:29,610
+there were also plenty
+of internal ones too.
+
+429
+00:13:29,610 --> 00:13:31,770
+Individuals not loyal to the revolution,
+
+430
+00:13:31,770 --> 00:13:34,140
+conspiring to bring about it's downfall.
+
+431
+00:13:34,140 --> 00:13:36,570
+Robespierre and the rest
+of the radical faction
+
+432
+00:13:36,570 --> 00:13:37,920
+were having none of it.
+
+433
+00:13:37,920 --> 00:13:39,360
+A new committee of public safety
+
+434
+00:13:39,360 --> 00:13:41,280
+was established with 12 members.
+
+435
+00:13:41,280 --> 00:13:43,290
+Its purpose was to protect
+the new French republic
+
+436
+00:13:43,290 --> 00:13:44,220
+from its enemies
+
+437
+00:13:44,220 --> 00:13:46,530
+and it basically became
+a 12 man dictatorship
+
+438
+00:13:46,530 --> 00:13:48,900
+with Robespierre as its leading voice.
+
+439
+00:13:48,900 --> 00:13:51,630
+The revolutionary tribunal
+was also reinstated.
+
+440
+00:13:51,630 --> 00:13:53,340
